First Man (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $44,936,545Details
International Box Office $60,587,192
Worldwide Box Office $105,523,737
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,003,728
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,439,428
Total $7,443,156
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of NASA’s mission to land a man on the moon, focusing on Neil Armstrong and the years 1961-1969. A visceral, first-person account, based on the book by James R. Hansen, the movie will explore the sacrifices and the cost—on Armstrong and on the nation—of one of the most dangerous missions in history.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$16,006,065 (35.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.81 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:42.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,640 opening theaters/3,640 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $58,994,530

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 12th, 2018 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: October 10th, 2018 (Wide) (Indonesia)
October 11th, 2018 (Wide) (Netherlands)
October 11th, 2018 (Wide), released as First man - El primer hombre (Spain)
October 11th, 2018 (Wide), released as Человек на Луне (Russia (CIS))
October 11th, 2018 (Wide) (Slovenia)
Video Release: January 22nd, 2019 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some thematic content involving peril, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2535 (Cert #51688), 7/11/2018)
Running Time: 141 minutes
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Astronaut, Astrophysics, Biography
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Temple Hill Entertainment, Universal Pictures, Perfect World Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The story of NASA’s mission to land a man on the moon, focusing on Neil Armstrong and the years 1961-1969. A visceral, first-person account, based on the book by James R. Hansen, the movie will explore the sacrifices and the cost—on Armstrong and on the nation—of one of the most dangerous missions in history.

Opening Weekend:$16,006,065 (35.6% of total gross)

Theater counts:3,640 opening theaters/3,640 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
